techniques.title: Assumption Spotlight
techniques.when: A plan depends on hidden beliefs, market guesses, or unproven user behavior.
techniques.prompt: Name the assumption, the cheapest disproof, and the decision that changes if it is false.

techniques.title: Tradeoff Map
techniques.when: The human is choosing between viable directions with different costs.
techniques.prompt: Compare options by user value, risk, effort, reversibility, and evidence.

techniques.title: Acceptance Slice
techniques.when: A broad idea needs a testable first version or implementation boundary.
techniques.prompt: State the smallest slice that proves the promise and the acceptance checks it must satisfy.

techniques.title: Experience Walkthrough
techniques.when: UX, onboarding, game feel, support flow, or human guidance quality is the main risk.
techniques.prompt: Walk one real user through the moment-by-moment experience, including failure and recovery states.

techniques.title: Evidence Ladder
techniques.when: Research, market, technical, or quality confidence is too vague.
techniques.prompt: Rank evidence from anecdote to proof, then choose the next source that changes the decision.

techniques.title: Risk Inversion
techniques.when: A plan looks plausible but weak proof could hide serious failure modes.
techniques.prompt: Assume this failed in production; identify the most likely cause and the prevention check.

techniques.title: Story Spine
techniques.when: A narrative, campaign, brand, or product promise needs a coherent arc.
techniques.prompt: Write the setup, pressure, turning point, payoff, and rejected alternate arc.

techniques.title: Constraint Remix
techniques.when: Brainstorming is stuck or producing generic options.
techniques.prompt: Change one constraint at a time: audience, time, budget, medium, rule, or forbidden pattern.

techniques.title: Implementation Trace
techniques.when: A decision needs to become stories, code, checks, and evidence.
techniques.prompt: Trace the decision through artifact, story, implementation step, validation command, and evidence.

techniques.title: Quality Crosscheck
techniques.when: Release confidence depends on requirements, risks, tests, and waivers lining up.
techniques.prompt: Map requirement to risk, test, evidence, owner, and waiver status.
